text_evidence: The anode feed gas is hydrogen with a flow rate of 20 fcl.
The anode feed gas is hydrogen with a flow rate of 20 fcl.
The cathode feed gas is carbon monoxide with a flow rate of 10 fcl.
The cathode feed gas is carbon monoxide with a flow rate of 10 fcl.
The working electrode is connected to the cathode.
The counter electrode is connected to the anode.
The reference electrode is placed in the middle electrolyte chamber.
The reference electrode is a mercury oxide electrode.
During the test, the middle chamber is filled with a circulating 1-molecule H liquid electrolyte.
The middle chamber thickness is 3 millimeter.

triple: continuous ionic PSV between cathode side am and anode side am
text_evidence: The circulating 1-molecule H provides a continuous ionic PSV between the cathode side am and anode side am.
The electrolyte flow rate is 30 fcl.

triple: shorten ion transparent distance, reduce ionic resistance, improve overall cell, reduce cell voltage
text_evidence: The purpose of reducing the chamber thickness is to shorten the ion transparent distance, reduce the ionic resistance and improve the overall cell and reduce the cell voltage.

triple: monitor electrode potentials microdifferentially and on-off operation
text_evidence: At the same time, the reference electrode allows to monitor the electrode potentials microdifferentially and on-off operation.

triple: constant current operation to monitor cell voltage stability during electrolysis
text_evidence: For this test, we will first perform a constant current operation to monitor the cell voltage stability during electrolysis.

triple: evaluate the resistance of the electrode after generation
text_evidence: After the test, we will perform the electrochemical impedance spectropase or PEIS to evaluate the resistance of the electrode after generation.
